summary: Lead accepted the M7 strategic terrain schema implementation after systems_architect writer REVIEW and independent qa_reviewer ACCEPT. The accepted diff defines StrategicTerrainDefinitionV1, deterministic terrain-route-node query surfaces, protocol/content-runtime/save-format boundaries, strategic terrain hash compatibility, ADR-011 implementation notes, and focused negative tests proving governance overlays, centroid proximity, hidden grid/lattice ids, and renderer-only lines cannot create route authority.

tests_run.result: VALID QA handoff after mechanical schema field completion preserving QA verdict.
format:check, lint, workspace typecheck, and architecture:check passed; architecture self-test reported 15 expected violations.
Focused deterministic and negative strategic terrain tests passed; 4 test files passed; 57 tests passed.
format:check, lint, workspace typecheck, and architecture:check passed; architecture self-test reported 15 expected violations.

tests_run.command: qa_reviewer: .\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run --config vitest.config.ts tests\sim-core-map-topology.test.ts tests\protocol-command-query-schema.test.ts tests\content-runtime-pack.test.ts tests\save-load-contract.test.ts
tests_run.result: Focused deterministic and negative strategic terrain tests passed; 4 test files passed; 57 tests passed.

decisions.decision: This acceptance does not approve M7 content lock or start M7 exit; downstream fixture, renderer-interaction, and validation tasks remain required.
Closed the completed systems_architect and qa_reviewer agent handles after ACCEPT while preserving thread-registry history.

risks.summary: Schema acceptance alone does not make the map product-acceptable; fixture/content, renderer-interaction, validation, and product-owner content-lock reconsideration remain required.
risks.mitigation: Keep M7 content lock rejected and promote only the next dependency-ready strategic terrain task after main integration.

requested_action: Open PR, wait for CI, merge to main, then close M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-SCHEMA-001 and continue to the next READY strategic terrain task.
